note: Analysis is based on the assumption that the CME is associated with Active Region 3712 which was at S25W91 (behind the limb) at the time. The fit in two coronagraphs corroborates this.

Measurement of faint leading edge using source location observed in UV imagery and best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to both faintness of the leading edge and a data gap in STEREO A COR2 lasting from 2024-06-24T15:38Z to 2024-06-24T23:53Z.
Measurement using the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement of somewhat messy leading edge based on source location and best fit between coronagraphs.
This CME was difficult to measure due to it's faintness in available coronagraph imagery.

Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT, also taking in account source location beyond the west limb. Due to the uncertainty in source location, the longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and candidate source location around longitude E50.
Measurement based on SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.

Measurement made mainly off of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery using the AR 3728 source longitude (with the observed southward deflection), with only one available STEREO COR2A frame making for an unideal triangulation.
Measurement made via triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO COR2A, finding some southward deflection from the source of AR 3719 (S17W42).

Measurement made via triangulation of the front between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, finding a fit roughly in the SE near the location of a cluster of ARs. The CME is notably faint and hard to follow in SOHO imagery due to the more direct angle, but STEREO imagery offers a better side-angle of the front that helps triangulate this as a front-sided longitude.
Measurement made using source location seen in UV imagery and best fit between coronagraphs. STEREO-A was in a campaign which affected STEREO-A COR2 difference imagery for this CME.
Measurement made using best fit between coronagraphs. STEREO-A was in a campaign which affected STEREO-A COR2 difference imagery for this CME.
Measurement based on source location as well as best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on source location as well as leading edge using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.

Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. Deflection in the field of view is possible, or a portion of the CME may not be measurable due to the CME's faintness.
Measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only due to lack of STEREO A COR2 imagery usable for this measurement.
Measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only, based on source location.
Measurement based on source location, with slight adjustment for best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. The CME may deflect in the field of view.

Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. There may be a fainter front, possible a shock, ahead of this measurement, but it was deemed not measurable with available imagery. STEREO A imagery is limited to 2 frames due to ongoing campaign.

Measurement based on potential source location on east limb using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only. The CME was not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 difference imagery and was not utilized for this measurement. Due to the uncertainty in the source location on the limb, the longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees.
Parameters based on best fit of CME features using source location. STEREO A COR2 imagery was not usable in measurement due to ongoing campaign impacting difference imagery.

These parameters are based off of the source of the CME and the best fit of the CME features in difference imagery. STEREO A COR2 difference imagery was not used in this measurement due to an ongoing campaign impacting image availability.

Measured longitude based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. There is only one frame available in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to an ongoing campaign, so the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to an ongoing STEREO campaign, only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery was available for this event. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Parameters based on apparent source location and best fit of CME features in SOHO LASCO C3 difference imagery.
Measurement taken using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs, which matched one of the two candidate source locations seen on UV imagery of the Earth-facing disk.
Measurement taken using best fit between coronagraphs and approximate source location based on UV imagery of the Earth-facing disk.
Measurement using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.
Measurement taken using best fit between coronagraphs and assessment that the location of the source is beyond the southwest limb of the Earth-facing disk based on UV imagery.
Measurement of the partially visible leading edge which precedes the core portion with the same shape. Measurement using best fit between coronagraphs.

Measured longitude based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event becomes diffuse and difficult to measure after a few early frames. This measurement relies on early imagery as a result.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured longitude deviates about 20 degrees west of the source location, possibly due to deflection from the source eruption.

Very approximate measurement because the source is likely behind (or on) the limb and because the CME front is relatively faint in coronagraphs. The fit with 2 coronagraphs favors the on-limb or behind the limb source, with the variability of longitude  from at least 90 to 110 degrees.

Very uncertain analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs and supported by a faint dimming between two active regions as a possible source. The CME front is very faint (not seen in SOHO LASCO C3 at all) and the source is very faint, resulting in the high uncertainty of analysis.
This analysis is based on the fit with two coronagraphs for the wider and slightly fainter more full-halo like shock front (vs the more partial halo bulk to the NW in SOHO).
Analysis based on the best fit of the more NE brighter front of this complex-shaped CME with two coronagraphs.
Approximate analysis based on the best fit in coronagraphs. Longitudes could range between approximately -100 and -110 degrees.
Analysis based on the location of the source (eruption in Active Region 3740) and confirmed by the fit of the front in two coronagraphs.
Measured using best fit, which yielded a longitude closer to W80 than originally estimated W90. Smaller eruption noted in CME note was located at approx. W80.

Filament spanned from E90 to likely several tens of degrees beyond the SE limb, as evidenced by UV imagery. Several attempts to measure were made, each time using longitudes that were progressively further beyond the E limb. Longitude E124 produced the best stereoscopic fit between coronagraphs.

Measurement of very faint leading edge, using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. Leading edge is too diffuse to track with confidence in SOHO LASCO C3 difference imagery.
Approximate analysis of the brighter narrower bulk front of this CME based on the best fit in two coronagraphs, somewhat confirmed by the potential source - a behind the ENE limb filament.
Approximate analysis of the fainter and wider likely shock front of this CME based on the fit in two coronagraphs.
Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO and STEREO A difference imagery. Measurable longitudes were between -90 to -80 degrees with speeds ranging between 700 km/s to 750 km/s.
Approximate analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. The source is unclear.

Analysis is based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. The fit points to the active region 3752 (N22E63) as the source of this CME, however all coronal signatures indicate a location close to the limb.
Analysis is based on the location of the associated flare and is confirmed by the fit with two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. Measured longitudes ranged between -80 to -95 degrees with speeds between ~850 km/s to ~930 km/s depending on the longitude used.
This measurement uses SOHO LASCO C2 imagery only due to the CME's faintness in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery. The CME was not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 difference imagery.
Updated measurement of the bulk leading edge of the CME using additional SOHO LASCO C3 imagery.
Measurement of the fainter, wider, potential shock feature of this CME, with slight adjustment to longitude for the CME fit in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only, based on source location.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

Analysis including both outermost lobes of the CME front together (including a diffuse nose seen in C2 that protrudes out from the main, bright loop), as they initially travel separately but catch up and travel together in the latest frames of C3. Using source of the filament on the limb at E90 and is not a plane-of-sky measurement.
Measurement of faint leading edge using best fit between coronagraphs.
A speculative measurement since the source has not been found and analysis is solely based on the fitting with two coronagraphs. Front-sided longitudes do not yield a fit with two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as source location.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location.
Triangulated measurement of this CME between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A given the uncertain source longitude, finding a good far-sided fit (and a poor on-disk fit) around E120-E140.

Well triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO COR2A using the source of AR 3744, finding a great fit at these coordinates. This measurement excludes C2 and some earliest C3 and COR2A frames to provide a more accurate final speed, as some acceleration was observed in the measurement with every frame.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, given the visible EUV source but unclear specific longitude. Despite only having a few available COR2A frames, this was enough to get a good fit around W85-W95, settling on W88 as the best fit.
These parameters are based off of the best fit of the suspected BULK of the CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO difference imagery. No STEREO A COR2 imagery is available for this CME.
Parameters based on the potential SHOCK feature using SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 difference imagery. There is uncertainty with this analysis due to lack of stereoscopic viewpoints.

Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured longitude may vary slightly as the source is far-sided and a precise source location is therefore unknown.

Measurement of the inner bulk feature based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.. Multiple measurements produced speeds ranging from 1277 km/s to 1544 km/s and longitudes spanning 170 to 176 degrees.

Measurement of the possible shock feature based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Multiple shock measurements produced speeds ranging from 1544 km/s to 1691 km/s. It is difficult to obtain a decent fit of the entire shock feature in both SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This measurement therefore focuses on the brighter, northern shock feature without compromising on the fit between coronagraphs.

Measurement using source location and best fit between a multitude of frames in C2/C3 and four frames of STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. Leading edge is extremely faint in STEREO A COR2 difference imagery, but the feature is just visible enough to track for a few frames.

Improved BULK measurement as SOHO data backfilled shortly after the prelim measurement (and prior to sending a notification from it). Better triangulated between SOHO and STEREO to the source of E77, and does not include the poor quality frames of SOHO LASCO C3 in the latest frames of this CME.

SHOCK measurement triangulated between SOHO and STEREO imagery. While the source is at E77, that does not visually fit swpc_cat well even in one coronagraph, and requires heavy deflection east or west to fit, favoring a front-sided fit around E30-E40. Some uncertainty lies in this shock analysis as a result.

Measurement based on a possible source location (filament eruption visible in SDO AIA 304 imagery centered near N30E80 at 2024-07-24T05:15Z). The measured parameters may vary slightly as there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available due to a data gap in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery at the time of this event.
Measurement using STEREO A COR2 imagery only due to lack of SOHO imagery. Longitude based on approximate source location.

Measurement of the wider, faint, faster shock feature ahead of the leading edge of the CME. This measurement using STEREO A COR2 imagery only due to lack of available SOHO imagery, and the longitude is based on the approximate source location.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraph imagery.

Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The northern part of the front that sticks out ahead of a smoother front closer to the bulk of the CME was excluded from the measurement due to fitting constraints.

Parameters based on the best fit of the bulk of the halo. Fainter portions of the wider halo visible to the south/southeast were excluded from this measurement. Different portions of the CME were analyzed to confirm far sided parameters.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT. The CME may deflect in the field of view from the source location.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement only using SOHO C2/C3 due to a STEREO COR2A data gap, but high confidence in the source of W85 based on SDO imagery and visual fit in swpc_cat.
Measurement based on the approx. source location and best fit between C2/C3 and COR2A. This CME was visible for only a few frames in C3 and COR2A before becoming diffuse.

Measurement based on the source location. This event is extremely faint/undetectable in COR2A difference imagery; the CME is more clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 white light imagery. The leading edge is also diffuse and disappears after a few frames of SOHO LASCO difference imagery.
Measurement based on the approx. source location and best fit between C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Since the source is observed near or beyond the western limb and there is no precise source location, the measured longitude may vary slightly.

Measurement made by initially lining up with STEREO COR2A imagery as it is more clear, using the rough center of the dimming ~S08E35 as a start and finding some deflection when triangulating as best as possible with SOHO LASCO C2/C3. Some speculation exists with how faint SOHO observes this CME, and not every frame distinctly sees the front, best seen early in C2 and late in C3.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO and STEREO, although the leading edge is notably faint in both coronagraphs leading to some uncertainty in the leading edge and full width.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO and STEREO, with good confidence in this fit compared to the outermost front of preceding CME 26/20:48Z, as this front appears much brighter in SOHO and STEREO coronagraph imagery following that separate front.
Parameters based on best fit of the bulk feature seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery.
Parameters based on shock feature in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. Longitude may vary by +/- 10 degrees based on analysis.
Measurement using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT, focusing on leading edge feature directed towards the NE as seen in STEREO A COR2.
The CME was partially obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3 difference imagery, complicating the measurement. The CME is faint in STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.

Approximate measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. The leading edge was not measurable in SOHO LASCO C3 and was partially obscured by the instrument pylon.
SHOCK measurement of wider, fainter shock feature ahead of the leading edge of the CME.
Updated bulk measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraph imagery.
Measurement based on source location near or on east limb and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement of leading edge based on source location and using the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Only 2 frames of STEREO A COR2 imagery were used for this measurement due to sparse imagery and poor image quality. This measurement primarily uses SOHO LASCO C3 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured longitude may vary slightly as the source is far-sided and there is no precise source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Because the source location is likely far-sided and unknown, the measured longitude may vary slightly.

Measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 using SWPC_CAT. There were a range of longitudes found in SWPC_CAT measurements due to the uncertainty of the source location on/beyond the SE limb.

Measurement based on probable source location near AR 3762 (S11W46) with a more Northern deflection visible. Leading edge was too diffuse to clearly track in STEREO A COR2, resulting in only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 being used for this measurement.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT and potential source location.
Measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 using SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the northern feature with a distinct leading edge that is narrower than the shock of the CME. Limited STEREO A COR2 imagery was available for this measurement due to data gaps.
Measurement of wider, fainter SHOCK feature based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Parameters based on source location and best fit of the bulk of the CME.
Parameters based on best fit of CME using the source location as a guide and visual inspection of directionality of the feature in the single STEREO A frame available during this event.
Parameters based on source location and best fit of features in SOHO LASCO C3 difference imagery.

Measurement made via triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, finding a great fit around N27W49 despite the lack of an EUV signature corresponding to these coordinates. Far-sided attempts to fit this CME yielded a poor triangulation, so this CME is front-sided with good confidence.
Triangulated measurement using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO COR2A using AR 3772 as the potential source, finding a good fit here.

Measurement based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery, there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available for this event and the measured longitude may vary slightly as a result.

Measurement based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery, there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available for this event and the measured longitude may vary slightly as a result.
Preliminary measurement of the fainter, faster, wider bulk of this CME. Only SOHO LASCO C2/C3  imagery was available for this measurement due to a data gap, so this measurement is approximate.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement of the narrower, northern portion of this CME, based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Due to poor difference imagery quality in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, this is an approximate measurement with limited imagery. Measurements ranged from 430 km/s to approximately 600 km/s.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery, there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available to confirm the measurement. Additionally, the northern portion of this event is partially obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

Measurement of the shock front using SOHO LASCO C3 imagery only. Because there is a data gap in STEREO A COR2 and this CME is partially obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the source location. This event is too faint to measure confidently in STEREO A COR2 imagery, so this measurement relies on SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery only. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

Measurement of the bulk portion based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. The measured longitude deflects about 15 degrees from the source location, and multiple measurements produced speeds ranging from 1092 km/s to 1120 km/s.
Measurement of the shock front based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Approximate measurement based on a fit with two coronagraphs. The source is behind the NE limb.

Re-analysis based on the interpretation of these two consequtive fronts as two separate CMEs with distinct sources seen behind the limb in the NE. Analysis is approximate, based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. Longitude can vary (+-10 degrees).

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A using AR 3775 as a source, finding a good fit deflected northward. This front is very faint and diffuses late in C2 and COR2A frames, and is barely visible in C3, so some uncertainly lies in tracking the true front as it fizzles.

BULK measurement, triangulated between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO COR2A using the source of the eruption from AR 3768 around S14W88 as the source, finding a good fit slightly deflected eastward. This CME front is a bit lopsided to the northern end, so this fit best tracking the leading edge misses some material on the southern portion of the leading edge.

Triangulated measurement at W90 between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, not an official plane-of-sky measurement. The distinct source longitude is not clear, but W90 triangulated well, so the longitude is likely within W80-W110 favoring front-sided due to the cluster of numerous active regions.
Measurement made using best fit between C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Many frames of both C3 and STEREO A COR2 were able to be used. Leading edge is faint, but trackable.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge of this event becomes too diffuse to measure after a few frames, so this measurement relies on early imagery.

Measurement based on the approximate source location. Because there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 during this event, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

A very speculative analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs, however there are only two images in STEREO COR2 that possibly show the front of this CME after the data gap and the front in COR2 is super faint. The source of this CME is behind the limb, so the longitude is very approximate.
Measurement based on the approx. source location and best fit between C2 and COR2A. Leading edge becomes diffuse after about 3 frames in C3 difference imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Analysis based on the location of the potential source of the flare and eruption - Active Region 3765 and confirmed by a good fit in two coronagraphs with this longitude. The source is behind the limb, but is seen on disk in EUVI STEREO A.
Measurement based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery, there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 with STEREO COR2A, using the source filament as a guide, finding some northward deflection.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2

Measurement based on best fit between multiple frames of C2, C3, and COR2A, as well as location of candidate source on the NE limb. Leading edge is faint in STEREO A COR2 and becomes diffuse before reaching halfway through C3 field of view.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on source location. Limited imagery was used in SOHO LASCO C3 due to the CME's faintness.
Measurement using fit on back side in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2. Because this is based on the best fit of the halo in SOHO only, the longitude is very approximate.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and a late frame of STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This event was too faint to be measured with SOHO LASCO C3.

Measurement based on source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3. Event is too faint to reliably track in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, and was extremely faint in SOHO LASCO C3, leading to a highly speculative measurement.
BULK measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
PRELIMINARY SHOCK measurement based on source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3. This feature was too faint to be reliably tracked in STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. There is no SOHO LASCO C3 data available for this measurement due to a data gap from 2024-08-09T02:24Z to 2024-08-09T04:39Z.
An approximate measurement based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. Weakly confirmed by a very faint dimming centered roughly around N30E35.

Measurement based on early frames of STEREO A COR2 imagery prior to a data gap and later frames of SOHO LASCO C3 imagery following an earlier data gap. Due to these gaps, the measurement is preliminary and approximate, based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
SHOCK measurement of the fainter, wider portion of this CME traveling ahead of the leading edge.
Approximate analysis of the narrower brighter bulk that is based on the best fit with two coronagraphs but gives a longitude close to the longitude of the location of the associated flare.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. The source is far behind the NW limb.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT. Similar parameters were also obtained in StereoCAT.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, finding a high-latitude far-sided fit. The CME becomes more dimpled and accelerates into later frames, so this fit attempts to best capture the motion of the leading edge.
Triangulated fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, though the front is notably more faint in COR2A and hard to track beyond 10 Rs.
Measurement of faint leading edge based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement of faint leading edge based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement of faint and diffuse leading edge based on best fit between C3 and COR2A, along with approx. source location seen in UV imagery.
A very approximate analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. The CME source is likely backsided.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2

Triangulated fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A using the source of N35E75 as a basis, finding some westward deflection in the fit. Half of the front fizzles away in early C2, so this measurement is of the more stable front in the eruption.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Two spacecraft measurement of the leading edge based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Approximate analysis based on the source location and confirmed by a good fit with two coronagraphs.
Approximate analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs and on the perceived center of the source filament.
Measurement assuming far-sided source given no major eruptions on the Earth-facing disk, possibly associated with GONG far-sided spots. Measurement incorporates SOHO C2 and C3 imagery only.
Measurement made estimating source latitude and longitude based on EUV imagery and use of SOHO C2/C3 coronagraph imagery.
Two spacecraft measurement using early frames of SOHO/STEREO A which considers multiple disjoint areas of outflow associated with the eruption as one bulk.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This CME had already overlapped with CME: 2024-08-14T04:12Z by the time it was visible in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement of the bulk portion based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Very uncertain analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. No clear on-disk coronal signatures have been found for this CME.

Measurement of the brighter, bulk portion based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Multiple measurements resulted in speeds ranging from 514 to 700 km/s.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Very uncertain analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. No clear on-disk coronal signatures have been found for this CME.
Very uncertain analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. No clear on-disk coronal signatures have been found for this CME.
Very uncertain analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. No clear on-disk coronal signatures have been found for this CME.

Best fit measurement of extremely diffuse leading edge. Only two frames of COR2A, two frames of C2, and one frame of C3 were able to be used due to the faintness of the event. However, the best fit parameters match the candidate source location well.
Measurement of extremely faint leading edge using best fit between coronagraphs.
Approximate analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. The CME seems to be more far-sided that the apparent source of this CME - a loop on/behind the NW limb seen in AIA imagery.
Measurement based on source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2. This event was too faint to be reliably tracked in C3 or STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Parameters based on the best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. Additional analysis with far-sided longitudes were completed, however the fits had more uncertainty with it compared to the fits using longitudes near AR13780.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A of early frames due to current availability. Deflected far northward of the eruption centered around N28E04 in SDO imagery, but it triangulated here between coronagraphs so it is in good confidence.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measured longitude based on the source location. This event is faint and is therefore not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery. This measurement relies on SOHO LASCO C2 imagery only and the measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Parameters are based on best fit of feature using the possible source with notable deflection to the NE.
A very approximate measurement based on a fit with two coronagraphs. No source has been found and fronts are very faint and fuzzy in both coronagraphs.
Measurement based on best fit between coronagraphs and location of source as seen in UV imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Because the source location is unclear and the leading edge is diffuse and difficult to measure, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Because the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Measurement of faint leading edge using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The leading edge becomes too diffuse to track just before reaching halfway through the C3 field of view. Several STEREO A COR2 images were able to be used, but poor quality/pixilation of STEREO A COR2 difference imagery after the frame timestamped 2024-08-19T15:38Z hindered proper tracking of the leading edge in these frames.
Parameters based on best fit of features in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement of faint leading edge using best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. There was some northern deflection seen especially in C2. The leading edge becomes too diffuse to track just before reaching the end of the COR2A field of view.
Measurement using best fit between C3 and COR2A.

Measurement of faint leading edge using best fit between C2, C3 and COR2A. The faint leading edge was easiest to track in C2 and COR2A, so these were mainly used to determine the best fit parameters. The leading edge is extremely faint and partially obstructed by the pylon in C3, but the faint leading edge feature is just distinct enough in C3 to be trackable.
Measurement using best fit between C3 and COR2A. The leading edge is brighter and more well-defined in COR2A than in C3, but there is still a very faint leading edge visible in C3.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO COR2A for a good far-sided fit despite the unclear source. This event becomes too faint and goes into the pylon in C3, giving no usable imagery.

These parameters are based off of a best fit of the CME in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. There are possibly overlapping features included in this analysis. The CME appears to speed up in later frames, so earlier frames were not included in this analysis.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STERO A COR2.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Because the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Leading measurement of the jet-like narrow CME using two frames of SOHO C3 and several frames of SOHO C2 as well as knowledge of the source latitude and longitude.
Measurement of bright leading edge using best fit between coronagraphs and farsided parameters as indicated by UV imagery.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
An approximate analysis based on the best possible fit with two coronagraphs. Front-sided longitudes don't produce a fit at all.
Measurement of two bright overlapping lobes that appear to travel together as one unit at the same rate, using best fit between coronagraphs and farsided parameters as indicated by UV imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and therefore unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Triangulated measurement with one usable frame of STEREO COR2A with SOHO LASCO C3, finding a good match at S42W24 despite the lack of an EUV-observed source at this time at this location. As such, this analysis is fairly speculative, but the best which can be done in real-time.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (since the source is backsided). Analysis focuses on two lobes of this CME, which move in unison.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. The CME had not developed fully in SOHO LASCO C2 frames and was not well representative of the overall features, so only C3 and COR2A were used for this measurement. A slightly faster, more narrow and fainter feature is also observed ahead of the main bulk of this CME but has been deemed inconsequential.

Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. A number of StereoCAT measurement were also taken yielding a lat/lon parameter range of 45-50/40-55 and a speed range of 330-363 km/s.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
An approximate measurement based on fitting of the front in two coronagraphs. Alternatively, the longitude could be higher, e.g. the longitude of 125 degrees is also produces a good fit.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and confirmed by the location of the likely source (filament eruption).

Analysis of the outer front of this CME based on the fit with two coronagraphs and confirmed by the location of the associated flare. There is also a fuzzy more inward narrower front, directed somewhat more southerward.

Best possible triangulation of this CME front with STEREO COR2A and limited SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery due to an ongoing SOHO campaign, using the source filament eruption as a basis and finding some deflection.

Best possible triangulation of this CME front with STEREO COR2A and limited SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery due to an ongoing SOHO campaign, coupled with the observed but unclear location of the far-sided source eruption in SDO imagery.

Best possible triangulation of this CME front with STEREO COR2A and limited SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery due to an ongoing SOHO campaign, coupled with the observed but unclear location of the far-sided source eruption in SDO imagery.
Parameters are based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery with a small portion of the asymmetrical lobe/front excluded due to measurement technique limitations.
Two spacecraft measurement of the CME leading edge measuring the most northwest portion in SOHO and northeast portion in STEREO A.
Updated StereoCAT analysis using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. The shock measurement yielded similar parameters, but a wider half-width.
Parameters based on fit using STEREOCAT of faint features. There is a higher degree of uncertainty with these parameters due to faintness of event and limited available frames.
Measurement taken using source location seen in UV imagery and best fit between coronagraphs.
Two spacecraft SOHO and STEREO A triangulation revealed that this CME was farther behind the eastern limb than was originally anticipated when comparing with SDO/GOES EUV imagery.
Leading edge measurement using two spacecraft and knowledge of the source location.
Approximate analysis based on the best fit in two coronagraphs. Longitudes could range from 135 to 150 degrees.
Measurement of the faster, wider, shock feature of this CME.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Measurement based on source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and a single frame of STEREO A COR2.

SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. No STEREO A COR2 imagery was used for this measurement as the shock was out of the field of view when data returned following data gap at 03/22:53Z
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. Leading edge was too faint to reliably track in SOHO LASCO C3.

Leading edge measurement using SOHO/STEREO A for triangulation focusing on the main loop of the CME and not a faint front that is seen beforehand; SOHO C3 not used because the CME is blocked by the pylon.
Shock measurement using SOHO C2, C3, and STEREO A, focusing on a more skillful fit of the shock in SOHO rather than STEREO A.
Updated bulk measurement with a smaller half-width.

Re-analysis with two coronagraphs (SOHO is now available) based on the best fit (which gives very precise longitudes with little variation (53-56 deg). No corresponding source found on the Earth-facing disk to confirm.
Two spacecraft measurement of the leading edge, using triangulation to determine the source, which appears to fit to the Earth-facing western limb or just beyond the western limb well.
One spacecraft measurement (SOHO) of the CME leading edge using the known source to fix the longitude.
Two spacecraft leading edge measurement using SOHO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 for triangulation of the latitude/longitude.
An approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. No source has been found in EUV imagery.
Analysis of the bright lobe of this CME based on the best fit in two coronagraphs and on the source location (location of the flare and of the dimming associated with it).
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on source location seen on the disk from the point of view of STEREO A EUV imagery as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on best fit between later frames of STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3 in SWPC_CAT.

Measurement made using best fit between coronagraphs. Leading edge is very faint in STEREO A COR2. Width of CME seems to gradually increase a bit as it progresses through C2's field of view and is influenced by surrounding outflow, then half the leading edge becomes too diffuse to measure in C3's field of view.
Parameters are based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery.

Measurement made using best fit between C2 and COR2A imagery, in addition to the candidate source location being farsided. Leading edge becomes diffuse before leaving the C2 field of view, but remains bright for most of STEREO-A COR2's field of view (24 degrees upstream of Earth).
Parameters based on the best fit of the bulk of the CME using StereoCAT. Measured speeds ranged between 920 km/s to 1100 km/s depending on fit.
Parameters based on best fit of the BULK of the CME. Data gap in SOHO misses CME front in SOHO LASCO C2 entirely.
Parameters based on best fit of the SHOCK of the CME. Portions of the shock are excluded due to measurement technique restraints.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Updated measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.

Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. The longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees. The CME speeds up in later frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
The leading edge of the CME fades away quickly in the imagery, so this measurement was made using early frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. Front was too faint to be reliably tracked in SOHO LASCO C3.

Measurement based on approximate source location in STEREO A COR2. There is only one available frame of SOHO LASCO C2 due to a data gap from 11/02:36Z to 11/07:12Z. Leading edge is too faint to be tracked in SOHO LASCO C3 by the time imagery has returned.

Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. There is no STEREO A COR2 imagery available for this measurement due to a data gap from 2024-09-11T14:38Z to 2024-09-11T22:23Z.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2. Front was too faint to be reliably tracked in SOHO LASCO C3 or STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Very approximate analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs and on the location of the source (flare).

Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. Parameters varied by approximately 85 to 104 degrees longitude, but speeds were consistently around 500 km/s.
Analysis based on a good fit with two coronagraphs confirmed by the location of the eruption. However, there appears to be a northward deflection of this CME.

Measurement using SOHO LASCO C3 imagery and only one frame of STEREO A COR2 imagery due to poor difference imagery quality. The longitude may vary by +/- 10 degrees, and this measurement is approximate.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. This measurement is approximate, and the longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees.

Leading edge measurement using one spacecraft (SOHO) to constrain the longitude, it was found that triangulation of the leading edge using two spacecraft was poor and did not determine a longitude close to the known source (fits using triangulation consistently fit to -85 to -100 degrees longitude).
Leading edge measurement primarily using later frames in SOHO C2 due to a data gap, eventually overtaken and washed out in coronagraph imagery by the 2024-09-14T04:23Z CME.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO/STEREO A COR2 imagery focusing on the brightest portion of the CME, so a lower bound on half-width.
Updated leading edge measurement of the bulk using only SOHO C3 imagery out to 18:30Z.
Updated SHOCK measurement based on source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 using more available imagery.
UPDATED measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 following SOHO LASCO data backfill. Front was too diffuse to be reliably tracked in C3.

Measurement made in SWPC_CAT using running difference imagery from STEREO A COR2. The source location is determined by corroboration with imagery from SOHO LASCO, which was rotated after SOHO keyhole maneuvers and unusable in SWPC_CAT.

Measurement made in SWPC_CAT using running difference imagery from STEREO A COR2. The source location is determined by corroboration with imagery from SOHO LASCO, which was rotated after SOHO keyhole maneuvers and unusable in SWPC_CAT.
Parameters based on best fit using correctly rotated SOHO LASCO C3 imagery (after midnight ZULU time) and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This event is fully obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A. Some deflection is observed in SOHO C2 and C3 as the leading edge is difficult to track in one line with the measuring tool, but regardless this front is clearly far-sided and out of the ecliptic.

Best fit using several frames of C2/C3 and COR2A, and source location as seen in SDO AIA imagery. The outermost 'layer' of this CME in COR2A difference imagery, i.e. the leading edge, becomes progressively more faint in later frames of COR2A, but can still be tracked as it remains relatively more visible near the legs of the CME.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, with the source observed in SDO on/near the NW limb.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and a farsided source location as observed in EUV imagery.
Measurement based on source location using SOHO LASCO C2 imagery.
